user_query:
I’m a cotton farmer preparing for my 2025 season, and I need both a quick financial analysis and polished communications for my buyer and lender. Using only the files in input/, please:

1) Financial analysis (read and extract from CSV/JSON):
- Parse input/expenses_2025.csv, input/production_2025.csv, and input/assumptions.json.
- Compute and save outputs/analysis/financial_summary.csv with exactly these columns:
  total_expenses_usd,
  total_production_lbs,
  total_bales,
  break_even_price_per_lb_usd,
  effective_contract_price_per_lb_usd,
  projected_revenue_usd,
  projected_net_income_usd.
- Definitions:
  • total_expenses_usd: sum of amount_usd in expenses_2025.csv.
  • total_production_lbs: sum(acres * expected_yield_lbs_per_acre) across fields.
  • total_bales: total_production_lbs / bale_weight_lbs from assumptions.json.
  • break_even_price_per_lb_usd: total_expenses_usd / total_production_lbs.
  • effective_contract_price_per_lb_usd: proposed_contract_price_per_lb_usd + expected_quality_premium_per_lb_usd from assumptions.json.
  • projected_revenue_usd: total_production_lbs * effective_contract_price_per_lb_usd.
  • projected_net_income_usd: projected_revenue_usd – total_expenses_usd.
- Also save outputs/analysis/price_scenarios.csv with three rows for effective price per lb at [base−0.05, base, base+0.05] where base = effective_contract_price_per_lb_usd. Columns: effective_price_per_lb_usd, projected_revenue_usd, projected_net_income_usd.
- Create a brief narrative report at outputs/analysis/summary.md that cites the key figures and explains what the break-even and margin mean.
- Rounding/units: currency values to 2 decimals (USD), prices per lb to 2 decimals (USD/lb), total_production_lbs to 0 decimals (lbs), total_bales to 2 decimals.

2) Rewrite two existing local drafts for clarity and tone using the computed figures (edit/rewrite documents and messages):
- Rewrite input/draft_buyer_email.txt into a concise, professional note (under 150 words) saved to outputs/messages/buyer_email_final.txt. Use the buyer name from assumptions.json, include the break-even price per lb and effective contract price per lb, and briefly justify pricing using the calculated totals. Keep a constructive tone and end with a clear next step.
- Rewrite input/loan_note_draft.md into a clear lender note saved to outputs/documents/loan_note_rewrite.md (target 180–220 words). Organize it with short headings and include: total expenses, total production (lbs and bales), break-even price per lb, projected revenue, projected net income, and a brief risk/timing section. Make it concise and readable.

Consistency check: Any figures quoted in the rewritten email and lender note must match the values in outputs/analysis/financial_summary.csv using the rounding rules above. Do not invent numbers not derivable from the inputs.

Input files are in input/; please create any missing output folders. Deliverables:
- outputs/analysis/financial_summary.csv
- outputs/analysis/price_scenarios.csv
- outputs/analysis/summary.md
- outputs/messages/buyer_email_final.txt
- outputs/documents/loan_note_rewrite.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
